User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the EKA Account system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

role.description: The role of the user (e.g., User, Therapist, Donor, Admin).

email.description: The email address of the user.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

lastLogin.description: Timestamp indicating the user's last login.

preferences.description: JSON blob containing user preferences such as dashboard layout and notification style.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number for Square customer matching.

id.description: Unique identifier for the session entity.

therapistId.description: Reference to User representing the therapist. (Relationship: User 1:N Session)

startTime.description: Timestamp indicating the start time of the session.

endTime.description: Timestamp indicating the end time of the session.

ratings.description: JSON blob containing session ratings (pain, mood, energy).

Donation.title: Donation
Donation.description: Represents a donation made through the EKA Account system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the donation entity.

donorId.description: Reference to User representing the donor. (Relationship: User 1:N Donation)

receiverId.description: Reference to User representing the donation receiver. (Relationship: User 1:N Donation)

currency.description: The currency of the donation (e.g., USD, EUR).

donationDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the donation was made.

isAnonymous.description: Indicates whether the donation was made anonymously.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a report generated within the EKA Account system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the report entity.

userId.description: Reference to User the report belongs to. (Relationship: User 1:N Report)

therapistId.description: Reference to User representing the therapist who created the report. (Relationship: User 1:N Report)

reportType.description: The type of the report (e.g., SOAP, User, Therapist).

reportDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the report was generated.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message exchanged within the EKA Account system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the message entity.

senderId.description: Reference to User representing the sender of the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

receiverId.description: Reference to User representing the receiver of the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

sentDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the message has been read.

id.description: Unique identifier for the subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to User the subscription belongs to. (Relationship: User 1:N Subscription)

planName.description: The name of the subscription plan (e.g., Free, Diamond).

startDate.description: Timestamp indicating the start date of the subscription.

endDate.description: Timestamp indicating the end date of the subscription.

status.description: The status of the subscription (e.g., Active, Cancelled).

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership: only the user (or an admin) can access their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores therapy sessions for each user. Path-based ownership: only the user (or their therapist) can access their session data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the session.

definition.description: Stores donation data. Includes denormalized 'donorId' and 'receiverId' into a 'members' map for authorization independence, supporting QAPs.

params.name: donationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the donation.

definition.description: Stores reports for each user. Includes 'therapistId' in each document to allow therapists to access reports they created for their patients.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the report.

definition.description: Stores messages. Includes denormalized 'senderId' and 'receiverId' into a 'members' map for authorization independence, supporting QAPs.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

definition.description: Stores subscription information for each user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the subscription.

definition.description: Stores all available therapies and services.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

definition.description: Stores all available VIP subscription plans.

params.name: planId
params.description: The unique identifier for the plan.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to align with the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, and Rules Are Not Filters (QAPs). It leverages Structural Segregation to ensure each collection has a homogeneous security posture and employs Access Modeling to standardize authorization patterns. Denormalization is used extensively to avoid `get()` calls in security rules, enabling atomic operations.  User data and user-owned data is segregated into `/users/{userId}`. Collaborative scenarios, like therapists and clients, use explicit membership maps.  Here's the breakdown by entity:

*   **Users:** User profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`. This allows path-based ownership, ensuring only the user or admins can access/modify their profile.
*   **Sessions:** Sessions are stored in `/users/{userId}/sessions/{sessionId}`. This ensures that only the user or their therapist can access their session data.
*   **Donations:** Donations are stored in `/donations/{donationId}`. The `donorId` and `receiverId` are denormalized to the `members` map for ease of authorization rule writing. This allows listing donations where the user is a donor or a receiver, enforcing QAPs.
*   **Reports:** Reports are stored in `/users/{userId}/reports/{reportId}`. The `therapistId` is included in each document to enable therapists to access reports created for their patients.
*   **Messages:** Messages are stored in `/messages/{messageId}`.  The `senderId` and `receiverId` are denormalized to the `members` map to allow easy authorization rule writing. This is a top-level collection enabling `list` operations (QAPs) based on membership without the need to get() other documents.
*   **Subscriptions:** Subscriptions are stored in `/users/{userId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}`, as subscriptions are specific to each user.
*   **Services:** A top-level `/services` collection is added to store all available therapy services. This is public or semi-public data, easily queryable by all users.
*   **VipPlans:** A top-level `/vipPlans` collection stores VIP subscription plan details. This is also public data for users to view.
